data.title: UK's Auction Technology surges after FitzWalter Capital raises buyout bid
data.description:
** Shares of Auction Technology up 11.2% at 357.5p

** London-based investment firm FitzWalter Capital says it has raised the possible cash offer for ATG to 400 pence per share from 360 pence

** Latest bid values British online auction operator at 491 mln pounds ($658.43 million).

** In 2025, stock fell ~50%

data.title: Dollar Could Fall Against Most Currencies This Year — Market Talk
data.description: The dollar looks set to depreciate against most currencies this year as lower U.S. interest rates make it cheaper to take protection against the risk of the currency weakening, Bank of America analysts say in a note. With U.S. rate differentials set to narrow further as the Federal Reserve cuts rates, the costs of hedging U.S. assets against a weaker dollar will also fall, they say. "We see still relatively high hedging costs as one of the key hurdles that may have prevented follow-through of hedging activity (of dollar exposures) in 2025." The Trump administration's push to lower housing related costs could encourage further rate cuts, they say. Fed independence risks also pose a potential dollar headwind. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Stock Idea: Zevra Therapeutics
data.description:
1) Clear Thesis

What they do: Zevra is a commercial-stage rare disease company. Its flagship drug, Miplyffa

(approved Sept 2024 for NPC), is now generating meaningful revenue, complemented by a massive

cash reserve.

Why it's mispriced: The market still treats ZVRA as a cash-burning biotech, ignoring its financial

transformation. With $230M in cash (boosted by a $150M PRV sale in 2025) and Miplyffa

generating ~$26M in Q3 2025 alone, the company is on the cusp of profitability. The stock

currently trades at just ~2 cash.

Path to 2: As Zevra reports its first profitable quarters in 2026, the valuation will re-rate from a

"cash shell" discount to a growth multiple (3-5 sales), driving the price toward $18+.

2) Valuation Anchor

Metric: EV/Sales Re-rate

Run Rate: Annualizing Q3 2025 revenue ($26M) suggests a $100M+ revenue baseline.

The Math: A conservative 3 EV/Sales multiple on $100M revenue = $300M Enterprise

Value.

Target: $300M EV + $230M Net Cash = $530M Market Cap (~$10/share floor). Growth

to peak sales projections supports analyst targets of $26.00 (almost 3 upside).

3) Catalysts (Next 12 Months)

Profitability Inflection (Q4 2025/Q1 2026): Analysts forecast positive EPS ($0.05) in

upcoming quarters. Confirming this transition will eliminate dilution risk and attract

fundamental investors.

EU Expansion: Recent distribution deals signal European market entry, unlocking a second

major revenue stream beyond the U.S.

4) Bear Case

Competitive Threat: IntraBio's Aqneursa also treats NPC. If Miplyffa loses significant

market share to this competitor, revenue growth could stall.

Execution Risk: Any stumble in the commercial rollout of Miplyffa or Olpruva could delay

the profitability timeline, burning through the cash buffer.

5) Guru Activity

Insider Buying (Late 2025): Directors Alvin Shih (bought ~20k shares) and John Bode

(bought ~5k shares) recently added to their positions.

Smart Money: Nantahala Capital Management increased its stake by ~34% in Q3 2025,

signaling high conviction.

data.title: ZAWYA-PRESSR: Huawei appoints Rajesh Nagpal as Vice President of Enterprise Business for UAE
data.description:
Dubai, United Arab Emirates: Huawei, a global leader in information and communications technology (ICT) infrastructure and smart devices, has announced the appointment of Rajesh Nagpal as Vice President of Enterprise Business for Huawei UAE, reinforcing the company’s continued investment in local leadership and its commitment to advancing enterprise digital transformation across the country.

Rajesh will lead Huawei’s Enterprise Business in the UAE, overseeing strategy, sales execution, and ecosystem development to support customers across key sectors including government, energy, financial services, transportation, and large enterprises. He will focus on expanding Huawei’s enterprise portfolio, strengthening partner collaboration, and enabling customers to adopt advanced cloud, data, AI, and networking solutions aligned with national digital priorities.

Rajesh brings more than two decades of experience in enterprise technology and business leadership, with a strong track record of driving large-scale transformation programs and building high-performing teams. Prior to joining Huawei, he held senior roles at Gulf Business Machines (GBM), where he partnered with CXOs and senior business leaders to translate technology investments into tangible business outcomes and long-term value.

Commenting on the appointment, David Tao, CEO of Huawei UAE, said: “We are pleased to welcome Rajesh Nagpal to Huawei. His deep understanding of the enterprise market and proven leadership across complex customer environments will play an important role as we continue to strengthen our enterprise business in the UAE. This appointment reflects our commitment to developing local leadership and supporting customers on their digital and intelligent transformation journeys.”

Highlighting his enthusiasm to join the Huawei team, Rajesh Nagpal said: “Stepping into this role at Huawei comes at a time when organisations across the UAE are accelerating their digital transformation journeys. Huawei has built a strong foundation in the UAE’s enterprise market through deep customer relationships and a robust partner ecosystem. I look forward to building on this momentum, working closely with stakeholders to help organisations modernize their operations and unlock new opportunities through digital and intelligent technologies.”

Under Rajesh’s leadership, Huawei Enterprise Group will continue to work closely with customers and partners in the UAE to accelerate industrial intelligence and support digital and intelligent economic development, helping create future-ready digital infrastructure across key industries. 

About Huawei

Founded in 1987, Huawei is a leading global provider of information and communications technology (ICT) infrastructure and smart devices. We have more than 208,000 employees, and we operate in more than 170 countries and regions, serving more than three billion people around the world.

Our vision and mission is to bring digital to every person, home and organization for a fully connected, intelligent world. To this end, we will drive ubiquitous connectivity and promote equal access to networks; bring cloud and artificial intelligence to all four corners of the earth to provide superior computing power where you need it, when you need it; build digital platforms to help all industries and organizations become more agile, efficient, and dynamic; redefine user experience with AI, making it more personalized for people in all aspects of their life, whether they're at home, in the office, or on the go.

For more information, please visit Huawei online at:

www.huawei.com

https://www.huaweicloud.com/intl/en-us/ 

Send us your press releases to pressrelease.zawya@lseg.com

data.title: Why Did ATOS Stock Jump 10% In Pre-Market Today?
data.description:
Key points:
  • ODD is granted to a drug or biological product intended to prevent, diagnose, or treat a rare disease or condition.
  • The designation provides Atossa with incentives such as tax credits for qualified clinical trial expenses, exemptions from certain FDA user fees, and potential for seven years of market exclusivity.
  • (Z)-Endoxifen is a targeted therapy developed to disrupt estrogen signaling by inhibiting and degrading estrogen receptors.

Shares of Atossa Therapeutics (ATOS) rose 10% in premarket trading on Friday, after the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) granted Orphan Drug Designation (ODD) to its investigational therapy (Z)-Endoxifen for Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy.

Importance Of ODD Approval

The FDA can grant ODD to a drug or biological product intended to prevent, diagnose, or treat a rare disease or condition.

The designation provides sponsors (Atossa) with several incentives, including tax credits for qualified clinical trial expenses, exemptions from certain FDA user fees, and the potential for seven years of market exclusivity following approval. ODD is a separate process from seeking approval or licensing.

Last week, the FDA issued a “Study May Proceed” letter for the company’s metastatic breast cancer trial under an IND for (Z)-endoxifen, a decision the company said supports expanding the drug’s use in ER+/HER2- metastatic breast cancer.

What Is This Investigational Drug?

(Z)-Endoxifen is a selective estrogen receptor modulator designed to inhibit and degrade estrogen receptors, including hormone-resistant cancers. The company is also exploring the drug’s broader therapeutic potential across additional disease pathways. The pharmaceutical company is developing an oral formulation designed to pass through the stomach unchanged, so it doesn’t become inactive.

How Did Stocktwits Users React?

Retail sentiment for ATOS on Stocktwits remained in the ‘bearish’ territory over the past 24 hours, despite the sharp premarket climb.

One user believes that the news could push the stock price above $1. It is currently at $0.6.

https://stocktwits.com/virviru/message/642015533

Atossa will hold a shareholder meeting on January 20 to propose, among other items, a reverse split of its common stock at a ratio of 5:1 to 20:1.

data.title: EvoNexus Group, Developer Of Social And Entertainment Mobile Apps Connecting Users Globally, Files for Nasdaq IPO
data.description:
EvoNexus Group filed for an initial public offering on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol “EVON.” The Hong Kong–based holding company runs a portfolio of social and entertainment mobile apps through its subsidiary, Mobclick. The company plans to sell 3,750,000 Class A ordinary shares at an expected price of $4.00 per share.

Business Description

EvoNexus Group is a Cayman Islands holding company that operates primarily through its Hong Kong subsidiary, Mobclick. The company develops, operates, and monetizes a family of social and entertainment-focused mobile applications designed to connect users worldwide through text, voice, and video. Its portfolio—available on Google Play—includes titles such as LivChat, BunchatLite, PeachU, Meeya, Meeya Lite, Jogan Pro, MeetClub, BakBak, UpChat, HoldU Pro, Timo, and RealCall. These apps integrate AI-based matching, real-time translation, and content moderation to facilitate cross-language communication and safer interactions.

EvoNexus generates revenue from three streams: advertising (third parties pay to display ads in-app), user recharge (paid access to premium features like chat, voice, and video), and other services (project-based UI and graphic design). The company emphasizes continued investment in product innovation, infrastructure, and new monetization features—such as virtual gatherings—while expanding its user base in target markets like India and Brazil.

Market Overview

  • Total addressable market: Global video-based social networking platforms were $2.48 billion in 2024
  • Market growth: Historical 2020–2024 CAGR ~7.9%; projected 2025–2029 CAGR ~5.7% to ~$3.30 billion
  • Market position: Focus on social and entertainment apps with user bases in India and Brazil
  • Key competitors: Milian Group, Bermuda Inc., TangoMe, Inc., Hyperconnect LLC, Party Game Pte Ltd
  • Industry trends: 5G rollout, AI-driven matching and moderation, broader mobile data access, and integrated online payments

Operational Metrics

  • Customers: Two customers represented ~28% and ~16% of FY2025 revenue; one customer represented ~33% in 1H FY2026
  • Locations: Headquarters in Hong Kong; offices leased in Hong Kong and Beijing
  • Geographic presence: Significant users and customers in India and Brazil, with revenue also from Hong Kong, Singapore, the U.S., and others
  • Partnerships: Distribution via Google Play; relies on third-party ad networks, cloud providers, and payment platforms
  • Other key metrics: FY2025 ARPU ~$9.1; FY2025 eCPM ~$9.7; FY2025 ad impressions per user ~3.67
  • Users (FY2025): ~5,350,174 total users; ~545,313 paying users; ~2,140,070 MAUs; ~449,415 DAUs
  • Users (Six months ended Aug 31, 2025): ~2,457,820 total users; ~236,403 paying users; ~2,182,871 MAUs; ~442,637 DAUs

Financials Highlights

  • Revenue (current): $11,572,003 (FY2025)
  • Revenue growth: Up 5.1% year over year (FY2025 vs. FY2024)
  • Gross profit: $10,279,873 (88.8% margin)
  • Operating income: $1,019,217 (income from operations, FY2025)
  • Net income: $929,201 (FY2025); $427,484 for six months ended Aug 31, 2025

Management

  • Chong Li, Director, Chairman & Chief Executive Officer - Former operations leader at Mobclick; prior roles in platform operations and content monetization; B.S. in Internet of Things Engineering.
  • Liu Liu, Chief Financial Officer & Director - Former senior manager at BDO China Shu Lun Pan CPAs; experience in financial planning, reporting, and compliance.
  • Zhenguo Wu, Independent Director Appointee - Capital markets and legal advisory background; roles in investment banking and corporate governance; J.D.-equivalent in law.
  • Yanxian Chen, Independent Director Appointee - Founder/CEO in data-driven decision platforms; prior roles in risk control and financing platforms; M.Fin., University of California, Riverside.
  • Lei Gu, Independent Director Appointee - Technology executive with experience in gaming and streaming; former CTO at Renren/Moatable; computer science training at Tsinghua University.

IPO Structure

  • Issuer: EvoNexus Group LTD
  • Filing date: January 15, 2026
  • Proposed ticker: EVON
  • Exchange: Nasdaq Capital Market
  • Price range: $4.00 per share (expected)
  • Offering size: $15.0 million (or $17.25 million with over-allotment)
  • Shares offered: 3,750,000 Class A Ordinary Shares (+562,500 over-allotment option)
  • Lead underwriters: Kingswood Capital Partners, LLC
  • Use of proceeds: Research & development, marketing and promotion, team expansion, and potential acquisitions

Original SEC Filing: EvoNexus Group LTD - F-1 - Jan. 15, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.
